title: Parallel tool dispatch: independent tool_use blocks execute concurrently
current_state: resp.tool_calls executes in a sequential for-loop. When the model requests 3 independent reads (weather + calendar + memory), latency is the SUM of all three.
target_state: Tool calls in one assistant message run via ThreadPoolExecutor (max_workers=4) with per-call timeout (default 60s); results reassemble in original tool_use order so the tool_result message is byte-identical in shape to today. Side-effectful tools (modify_file, create_file, execute_bash, execute_python, computer_*, gmail_send, telegram_send) are marked serial=True in their ToolSpec and force the whole batch sequential — correctness beats speed when writes are involved.
migration_plan: 1. Add ToolSpec.serial: bool = False; set True on every write/side-effect tool (enumerate explicitly, default-closed for execution tools).
2. In the tool loop: if len(tool_calls)>1 and all are serial=False → ThreadPoolExecutor, ordered collect; else today's loop.
3. Per-call timeout returns a structured timeout error as that call's tool_result (turn survives).
4. Thread-safety audit of shared tool singletons (browser/_b(), computer-use); mark non-thread-safe ones serial=True.
5. Tests: 3 fake slow tools → wall-clock < sum; ordering preserved; one serial tool in batch → sequential path; timeout produces error result not exception.
risk_notes: RISK_FLAGGED files → interactive. SQLite connections are per-call in most handlers (check _bump_access). Default-closed: anything ambiguous gets serial=True.
